what_it_does: Documents a registry-driven design proposal for the graph instrument's layout, orbit geometry, and coil/uncoil motion, including build phases and open decisions for Tim.
observations: States a mandate that graph positioning and animation derive from registry grammar, resolution, and temporal faces rather than handwritten constants.
Identifies current graph layout constants, duplicated graph-view constants, and margin-relative child positioning as items to replace.
Maps sibling gaps, depth gaps, containment cascade, type axes, and optional frame fractions to cited registry rows and resolver paths.
Proposes parent-relative cascade layout, type-perpendicular child stacking, exported graph positions, and resolved orbit geometry.
Frames motion as missing a runtime resolver twin comparable to spacing, then proposes a coil behavior record, DNA.motion, and injectMotion.
Distinguishes the existing static coil end-frame from the absent uncoil transition.
Recommends a transient FLIP/overlay animation phase while explicitly leaving persistent keyed reconcile as an architecture fork.
Includes ordered build phases for positioning, motion resolver work, and coil/uncoil implementation.
Ends with explicit OPEN-FOR-TIM questions about motion architecture, bond-word mapping, type stacking, collapse symmetry, and change-intensity.
standouts: The document treats exported node positions as a shared seam for layout deduplication, camera framing, and motion endpoints.
The motion proposal is grounded in address continuity: orbit dots and landed child nodes are matched by child id, not by visual index.
The implementation order puts static end-frame geometry before animation, citing end-frame law as the reason.
conventions: Uses cited file:line references throughout to anchor design claims.
Separates diagnosis, positioning, motion, reuse patterns, build steps, and open decisions into numbered sections.
Marks unresolved choices as OPEN-FOR-TIM rather than presenting them as settled.
Uses registry vocabulary such as bonds, cascade, temporal face, anchor, and diff as organizing terms.
Combines design rationale, implementation notes, and migration sequencing in one command document.
concerns: Several implementation choices remain intentionally unresolved inside the file, including bond-word mapping and final type-stack geometry.
The document is dense and mixes architectural argument with step-by-step build instructions, which may make it harder to use as a direct task checklist.
Some proposed behavior depends on new seams and resolver APIs that the file says do not yet exist.
notes: Briefing note: this file is a design command for making the graph's layout and coil/uncoil motion resolve from registry data rather than graph-local constants.
questions: Which bond words should map to sibling and depth gaps?
Should the first implementation use transient overlay motion or persistent keyed reconcile?
Is the proposed type-perpendicular stack the intended end-frame geometry?
Should collapse reverse the uncoil transition or commit instantly?
Should change-intensity become an active motion dial now or remain deferred?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it is a registry-driven graph design proposal with diagnosis, architecture options, ordered build steps, and open questions.
apparent_themes: resolver parity across color, spacing, and motion
derived constants instead of handwritten graph values
intention_signals: It seems intended to turn Tim's graph motion and layout direction into a traceable implementation plan while preserving unresolved design choices as explicit decisions.
novelty: Distinctive elements include treating graph orbit dots as address-keyed shared elements, deriving motion behavior through a temporal resolver, and using the same exported position map for static layout, camera framing, and transition endpoints.
contribution: This file contributes a neutral design ledger for converting the graph instrument from handwritten SVG layout and CSS timing literals into registry-resolved positioning, orbit geometry, motion tokens, and address-keyed transition behavior.
summary_for_embedding: A design command document proposing registry-derived graph layout, type stacking, exported positions, motion resolver APIs, and coil/uncoil overlay animation with explicit open decisions.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to guide implementation of graph positioning and animation so they derive from shared grammar, resolution, and motion records rather than graph-local constants.
